jokes.text: Listen, I was trying to buy this hand-crafted mahogany wood model of a B-25 Mitchell Panchito aircraft. - Aw, for me? - Don't sass me.
jokes.setup: Ron explains his oddly specific aircraft model purchase
jokes.punchline: April's fake sweetness followed by Ron's gruff dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron/April dynamic - his overly specific masculinity interests vs her deadpan sarcasm

jokes.text: And I went to this website, and this ad popped up that said 'Hey, Ron Swanson! Check out this great offer.' What's your question? My question is, what the hell?
jokes.setup: Ron describes targeted advertising
jokes.punchline: His complete bewilderment at basic internet tracking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Ron's technophobia meets modern reality in a perfectly believable way

jokes.text: Dude, if you think that's bad, go to Google Earth and type in your address.
jokes.setup: Ron is already freaked out about cookies
jokes.punchline: April casually mentions something even more invasive
jokes.explanation: April perfectly escalates Ron's paranoia with casual delivery

jokes.text: I am, of course, shocked... And not just because Ben usually prefers tall brunettes.
jokes.setup: Chris expressing shock about Ben and Leslie's relationship
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate comment about Ben's dating preferences
jokes.explanation: Chris's boundary-crossing oversharing disguised as supportive commentary

jokes.text: Are you hugging me, or are we fighting? Are you hugging or fighting? Let me know.
jokes.setup: Unclear physical interaction between Leslie and Chris
jokes.punchline: Leslie's confusion about what's happening
jokes.explanation: Awkward physical comedy through dialogue, captures the uncomfortable moment

jokes.text: I told you we'd feel better.
jokes.setup: Chris just announced an ethics investigation
jokes.punchline: His cheerful assertion they'd feel better after terrible news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment - toxic positivity after delivering bad news

jokes.text: Well, I wanted to get here early, you know, get a feel of the room. - So you got here-- - 3:00 A.M.
jokes.setup: Leslie arrived early for her hearing
jokes.punchline: Reveals she arrived absurdly early at 3 AM
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie overachievement taken to absurd extreme, perfectly timed reveal

jokes.text: Well, I wanted to sneak in before you got here-- an absurd pipe dream I now realize was impossible.
jokes.setup: Ben tried to arrive before Leslie
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges the impossibility of out-early-ing Leslie
jokes.explanation: Ben's self-aware commentary on Leslie's work habits

jokes.text: Aw, thank you so much for making my life so wonderful. - You're welcome. - I was talking to him. - Of course. - But also to you.
jokes.setup: Leslie thanks someone for the Li'l Sebastian toy
jokes.punchline: She was talking to the toy, not Ben (but also Ben)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie priority inversion - Li'l Sebastian comes first, then humans

jokes.text: For me, a slap on the wrist is like a kick to the nuts.
jokes.setup: Leslie worries about getting in trouble
jokes.punchline: Compares minor punishment to major physical pain for her character
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Leslie's perfectionist anxiety - any blemish is catastrophic to her

jokes.text: So, if you miss me, you just look at this... Wrinkled, hideous monster and you'll know I'm sitting there rooting for you, okay?
jokes.setup: Ben giving Leslie something to remember him by
jokes.punchline: Describes himself as a 'wrinkled, hideous monster'
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment with self-deprecating twist, classic Ben neurotic humor

jokes.text: His nickname was 'Old Stone Face' because of his steely demeanor and because he got in an accident at the rock quarry and dynamite blew up his face.
jokes.setup: Explanation of lawyer's intimidating nickname
jokes.punchline: The literal reason is much more disturbing than expected
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation from metaphorical to literal, quintessential Pawnee absurdity

jokes.text: Well, you're radiating pure joy. I went to my herbalist and got two B-12 shots. And then I ate an unreasonable amount of St. John's wort, and my herbalist took this weird bee pollen paste rubbed it around my gums. And now my mouth feels like a spaceship.
jokes.setup: Chris claims to be miserable but looks happy
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of absurd herbal treatments ending with 'mouth feels like a spaceship'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly escalating absurdity, perfect Chris health obsession, 'spaceship mouth' is wonderfully weird

jokes.text: In 1849, Sarah Nelson Quindle exposed her elbow outdoors, which was a class 'A' felony. Although she felt the law unjust, she acknowledged that she had broken it, and she nobly accepted her punishment-- to be set adrift on Lake Michigan like a human popsicle.
jokes.setup: Historical parallel about accepting unjust punishment
jokes.punchline: Absurdly harsh punishment for showing an elbow
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pawnee historical absurdity, 'human popsicle' is a great image

jokes.text: The night we returned was the first time we kissed each other on each other's mouths. It was excellent. That was unnecessary to add. I'm sorry. I'm nervous.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing when she and Ben first kissed
jokes.punchline: Overly specific description followed by oversharing and self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Leslie's nervous oversharing creates authentic cringe moment

jokes.text: I received adorable nicknames and amazing back rubs. Oh, you mean professionally. No.
jokes.setup: Asked about professional special treatment
jokes.punchline: Initially misunderstands as personal benefits
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection with Leslie's characteristic oversharing

jokes.text: Andy, I need evidence. - Got it! Wait! You don't know what evidence. Ah, right on. Yes. Hit me.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking Andy to get evidence
jokes.punchline: Andy's enthusiastic agreement before knowing what he's agreeing to
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character moment - eager to help without understanding

jokes.text: Where are you looking? Look here, focus here. Get the files in the drawer, and then get all the file boxes in the office. Put the files in the drawer. Let's go. - No. - Okay, I'll go with him.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving detailed instructions to Andy
jokes.punchline: Andy's complete failure to follow simple directions
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy through dialogue, shows Andy's lovable incompetence

jokes.text: I don't know the names of the other department heads. I'll go with him. Seriously? With the men in this office?
jokes.setup: Ron admits he doesn't know other department heads
jokes.punchline: Leslie's exasperation with the men's incompetence
jokes.explanation: Leslie's frustration with workplace gender dynamics

jokes.text: But when you sit back and let your reputation be destroyed. You go down in history as a frozen whore.
jokes.setup: Leslie referencing the historical woman from earlier
jokes.punchline: Blunt description of how history would remember her
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with escalation, 'frozen whore' is hilariously blunt and quotable

jokes.text: Don't silence him. Just make sure he can't talk.
jokes.setup: Leslie clarifying her instruction to April
jokes.punchline: Contradictory instruction that means the same thing
jokes.explanation: Leslie's attempt to sound less threatening while saying the same thing

jokes.text: That's Y-A and 18 'Y's and 44 exclamation points.
jokes.setup: Describing an email titled 'yay'
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific count of Y's and exclamation points
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie enthusiasm quantified, shows her obsessive attention to detail

jokes.text: In a world on the brink of financial meltdown... In a town unlike any other... An adorable man with a cute face and the future president of the United States-- what?--
jokes.setup: Movie trailer style voice-over
jokes.punchline: Leslie's grandiose claims about Ben interrupted by her own surprise
jokes.explanation: Leslie's over-the-top romantic view of Ben in movie trailer format

jokes.text: Ann! Ben and I hooked up last night! Aah! And I learned how to use iMovie. Call me later! Bye!
jokes.setup: Video message about hooking up with Ben
jokes.punchline: Equally excited about learning iMovie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie priorities - romantic milestone equals learning new software

jokes.text: Did Tom Ford turn around the House of Gucci? I do not know, but I'll assume that that is a yes.
jokes.setup: Asked if Leslie is a good employee
jokes.punchline: Answers with fashion industry reference he doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Tom's fashion-obsessed worldview applied inappropriately

jokes.text: How long were you two lovers? - Excuse me? - Oh, God.
jokes.setup: Chris asking about Leslie and Tom's relationship
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate use of 'lovers' and Tom's horror
jokes.explanation: Chris's inappropriate language choice creates awkwardness

jokes.text: No, that would be like dating my older sister's elderly aunt.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining why he and Leslie never dated
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily specific and harsh comparison
jokes.explanation: Tom's shallow age-obsessed perspective, slightly mean-spirited

jokes.text: I don't know why Leslie Knope's on trial. Ethel Beavers did it! - Beavers did it! - Sit down.
jokes.setup: Character witness defending Leslie
jokes.punchline: Blames court stenographer for everything
jokes.explanation: Absurd scapegoating of background character

jokes.text: I will hold myself in contempt of the court!
jokes.setup: Disruptive citizen in courtroom
jokes.punchline: Threatens to hold himself in contempt
jokes.explanation: Absurd legal misunderstanding, self-imposed punishment

jokes.text: Is this about the laptop? I didn't mean to steal it. I took it home, and I spaced. I forgot. I'm gonna bring it back. It totally works, but I got spaghetti in the keyboard.
jokes.setup: Jerry called as character witness
jokes.punchline: Immediately confesses to unrelated laptop theft with spaghetti damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry moment - accidentally incriminating himself, spaghetti detail is wonderfully specific

jokes.text: Um, khakis and button-down shirts-- your basic white people clothes.
jokes.setup: Describing what Ben and Leslie were wearing
jokes.punchline: Donna's blunt racial observation about their clothing
jokes.explanation: Donna's matter-of-fact social commentary, great economy of language

jokes.text: Oh, hell, no. I have ruined my share of hotel rooms, and trust me, nothing sexual happened in that room.
jokes.setup: Asked if anything romantic happened in the hotel room
jokes.punchline: Donna's expertise in hotel room destruction
jokes.explanation: Donna's confident sexuality and expertise, great character moment

jokes.text: I've seen over 200 episodes of Law & Order, and it's paying off big-time.
jokes.setup: Leslie feeling confident about her courtroom performance
jokes.punchline: Credits TV legal knowledge
jokes.explanation: Leslie's over-confidence from pop culture knowledge

jokes.text: And by the way, we got the gramps-- grants. God! Oh. It was gonna be such an awesome moment.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to make a dramatic courtroom statement
jokes.punchline: Flubs the word 'grants' and ruins her moment
jokes.explanation: Leslie's perfectionism meets human fallibility at worst moment

jokes.text: Is it ethical for a court stenographer to bet on the outcome of a hearing? You tell me. You're the one on trial.
jokes.setup: Leslie questions Ethel's ethics about betting
jokes.punchline: Ethel turns it back on Leslie who's literally on trial for ethics violations
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal, Ethel's deadpan delivery of harsh truth

jokes.text: Stop, please. I don't like to give out my address to anyone, much less have it on an official record.
jokes.setup: Ron being asked about his cabin address
jokes.punchline: Extreme privacy paranoia interrupting legal proceedings
jokes.explanation: Ron's paranoia escalating to obstruct justice, perfect character consistency

jokes.text: 9301 Cedarcrest Dr-- - Beep! I didn't hear that. 9301 Cedarcrest Drive!
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to get Ron's address on record
jokes.punchline: Ron making beeping sounds to block it, Leslie shouting it louder
jokes.explanation: Escalating physical comedy, perfect Leslie/Ron dynamic of opposing forces

jokes.text: I can smell the sulfur coming off her cloven hooves.
jokes.setup: Ron describing his ex-wife Tammy's arrival
jokes.punchline: Describes her as literally demonic
jokes.explanation: Ron's poetic hatred of Tammy, great supernatural imagery

jokes.text: May I remind you that you are under oath, and if you lie, I will fire you and have you prosecuted. Nothing. They will definitively prove nothing. You cut me off. I don't have any evidence.
jokes.setup: Tammy claiming to have damaging evidence
jokes.punchline: Chris's threat causes immediate backdown
jokes.explanation: Good structure - builds tension then deflates, Chris's rare moment of authority

jokes.text: Oh, interesting tidbit-- I once bribed him to keep my relationship with Ben quiet.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing maintenance worker George as unimportant
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions committing the exact crime she's accused of
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Leslie casually confessing while trying to minimize the witness

jokes.text: Yeah! I got a facial. My wife said my skin was luminous.
jokes.setup: George confirming he used the spa gift certificate
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic endorsement of facial treatment
jokes.explanation: Unexpected masculinity subversion, 'luminous' is a great word choice

jokes.text: I could read this if I weren't so blind.
jokes.setup: George asked to read without glasses
jokes.punchline: Reads a sign about being too blind to read
jokes.explanation: Clean ironic setup, undermines his credibility as witness

jokes.text: I would like to request a 55-day recess.
jokes.setup: Leslie needs time to research defense
jokes.punchline: Requests absurdly long recess
jokes.explanation: Leslie's desperation meets her precision with oddly specific number

jokes.text: Okay, mine doesn't have any pictures.
jokes.setup: Everyone reading law books for loopholes
jokes.punchline: Andy disappointed his book lacks pictures
jokes.explanation: Simple Andy character moment, predictable but fits

jokes.text: In 1856, the city council banned all sexual positions except for missionary. And two years later, they banned missionary.
jokes.setup: Reading absurd historical laws
jokes.punchline: Escalating sexual restrictions to complete ban
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure, absurd Pawnee puritanism taken to logical extreme

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Black people still can't legally use city sidewalks.
jokes.setup: Reading more historical laws
jokes.punchline: Discovers horrifying racist law still on books
jokes.explanation: Dark commentary on forgotten institutional racism, Andy's innocent delivery

jokes.text: Any woman caught laughing is a witch. That's true.
jokes.setup: April reading absurd misogynistic law
jokes.punchline: Andy's deadpan agreement
jokes.explanation: Andy's oblivious agreement with absurd sexist law, April's deadpan delivery

jokes.text: When a good person does something bad, they own up to it. They try to learn something from it, and they move on.
jokes.setup: Leslie calling herself a bad person
jokes.punchline: Ron's wise but understated moral philosophy
jokes.explanation: Ron's rare moment of emotional wisdom, perfectly understated delivery

jokes.text: Bribing someone to hide a sexcapade-- I'm proud to call you a friend.
jokes.setup: Leslie apologizing for letting everyone down
jokes.punchline: Tom's twisted values make him prouder of her corruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - shallow values make corruption seem cool

jokes.text: 'Cause your actions wounded me to my core, which is not easy since the bulk of my workouts are focused on core strengthening.
jokes.setup: Chris expressing emotional pain from Leslie's actions
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'wounded to the core' with fitness reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment - fitness obsession invades emotional expression

jokes.text: This is how I fight depression. Okay, fine. I'll stop.
jokes.setup: Ben asking Chris to stop jumping during serious conversation
jokes.punchline: Chris explains jumping is his depression management
jokes.explanation: Callback to Chris's mental health management, visual comedy through transcript

jokes.text: Was all of this-- all the sneaking around, the scandal, losing your job-- Was it worth it? Yes. It was.
jokes.setup: Chris asking if the relationship was worth all the trouble
jokes.punchline: Ben's simple, immediate affirmation
jokes.explanation: Understated romantic moment, Ben's certainty is touching and funny

jokes.text: I'm literally crying and jumping. Crying noise, crying noise, nose blow.
jokes.setup: Chris moved by Ben's love declaration
jokes.punchline: Transcript literally describes his emotional sounds
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about transcript format, Chris's emotional overreaction documented clinically

jokes.text: Actually, my real name is Gary. Gary? On my first day here, the old director-- he called me Jerry, and I just didn't think I should correct him.
jokes.setup: Jerry being called by wrong name
jokes.punchline: Reveals his actual name is different and he's been too polite to correct it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character revelation - explains years of abuse, perfectly pathetic Jerry moment

jokes.text: Gary Gergich? Jerry Gergich. Gary Gergich. Jerry-- God, they're both horrible. But Jerry's better. I'm gonna call you Jerry.
jokes.setup: Learning Jerry's real name is Gary
jokes.punchline: Tom decides both names are terrible but sticks with Jerry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom cruelty - even when learning the truth, chooses to continue the abuse

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I can't get over the Gary/Jerry thing. Neither can I. Jerry, you can go. We need a five-minute break.
jokes.setup: Gary/Jerry revelation disrupting proceedings
jokes.punchline: Even Chris needs a break to process this information
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the Gary/Jerry revelation's impact on everyone
